issues.comment: Using `forEach` with an `async` callback causes fire-and-forget behavior. `Array.prototype.forEach()` does not await async callbacks — it ignores the returned Promises and returns immediately. This means: (1) calendar/video deletions may not complete or execute at all, (2) the surrounding `try/catch` is dead code since no rejection from async callbacks can be caught, and (3) subsequent code runs before deletions complete, creating a race condition. Should use `await Promise.all(array.map(async ...))` or `for...of` instead.

Same pattern as vital/lib/reschedule.ts: `forEach` with `async` callback does not await the promises, making the `try/catch` dead code and causing calendar/video deletions to be fire-and-forget. Should use `await Promise.all(array.map(async ...))` or `for...of`.

Same pattern: `forEach` with `async` callback does not await the promises, making the `try/catch` dead code and causing calendar/video deletions to be fire-and-forget with potential race conditions. Should use `await Promise.all(array.map(async ...))` or `for...of`.
